label: GitHub Copilot private beta
description: First widely-used AI coding assistant; generates code completions inline in VS Code.

label: ChatGPT public launch
description: OpenAI's ChatGPT reaches 1 million users in 5 days. Marks the threshold at which LLMs become mainstream tools, including for security research.

label: Bing Chat (GPT-4 integrated)
description: Microsoft integrates GPT-4 into Bing, beginning mass deployment of frontier AI to general users.

label: GPT-4 public release
description: Significant capability step-change. First model to pass bar exam at ~90th percentile and demonstrate advanced code reasoning.

label: Google Bard GA
description: Google releases Bard publicly, intensifying LLM competition and further democratizing access.

label: GitHub Copilot Chat GA
description: Copilot gains conversational coding interface. Security researchers begin using it for vulnerability analysis.

label: WormGPT / FraudGPT reported
description: First documented attacker-optimized LLMs appear on dark web forums, specifically marketed for phishing email generation and exploit assistance.

label: Claude 2
description: Anthropic releases Claude 2 with 100k context window — first viable frontier competitor to GPT-4.

label: GPT-4 Turbo
description: Cost drops ~3x from GPT-4. Lower cost substantially reduces the economic barrier for AI-assisted attacker automation.

label: Gemini 1.5 Pro
description: Google's Gemini 1.5 Pro introduces 1M context window — large enough to analyze entire codebases for vulnerabilities in a single prompt.

label: Claude 3 Opus
description: Anthropic releases Claude 3 family. Opus exceeds GPT-4 on multiple benchmarks including code analysis.

label: GPT-4o
description: GPT-4 level capabilities at reduced cost and higher speed. Significantly lowers cost of API-driven security automation.

label: GitHub Copilot Workspace
description: AI begins autonomous multi-file code editing tasks without human guidance at each step — first agentic coding capability at scale.

label: Claude 3.5 Sonnet
description: Outperforms Claude 3 Opus on most tasks at lower cost. Rapid capability iteration continues.

label: OpenAI o3
description: Reasoning model with qualitatively improved code and mathematics. First model to score >85% on competitive programming benchmarks.

label: Agentic AI security tools (first enterprise deployments)
description: First generation of autonomous AI penetration testing tools reach enterprise buyers. AI agents begin conducting vulnerability research without continuous human supervision.

label: AI-generated CVE filings documented
description: Multiple CNAs begin publicly acknowledging use of AI tools in their vulnerability discovery pipelines, creating a measurable CNA attribution shift.

label: Autonomous AI security agents (widespread)
description: AI agents operating with minimal human oversight in both offensive (red team) and defensive (SOC) roles become common in large organizations.
